title: Crispy Parmesan Chicken with Garlic Herb Couscous
description: HelloFresh Hall of Fame — golden-crusted chicken cutlets coated in a panko, parmesan, and paprika crust, served over fluffy garlic herb couscous with lemony carrots on the side. Simple, satisfying, and endlessly popular.
instructions: Pound chicken breasts to ¾-inch thickness between plastic wrap. Pat dry and season with salt and pepper.
Mix panko, parmesan, and paprika in a shallow bowl. Press each chicken breast firmly into the mixture to coat both sides.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 4–5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (165°F). Work in batches if needed.
Meanwhile, peel and slice carrots into thin coins. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, a pinch of salt, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Roast at 425°F for 15 minutes or sauté in a separate pan until tender.
Make the couscous: bring chicken broth to a boil with butter and minced garlic. Remove from heat, stir in couscous, cover, and let stand 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork and stir in chopped parsley.
Serve chicken over garlic herb couscous with lemony carrots on the side. Garnish with extra parsley and lemon wedges.
tips: Pounding the chicken to an even thickness is the key to getting a crispy crust without overcooking.
Press the panko mixture firmly onto the chicken — don't just dip it. The more it adheres, the crispier it gets.
Swap couscous for rice or mashed potatoes if preferred.
Leftovers are great sliced cold over a salad the next day.
